title: The Great War (1914-1918)
gmStyle: Second person, present tense. Grounded, sensory, human-scale. NPCs have names, voices, and wants of their own. Scenes are 2-4 paragraphs and end on a hook, a choice, or a question.

ratingOverlays.story: Danger is real but violence reads like a classic adventure novel: courage, close calls, and consequences without gore. Deaths happen off-page and are honored, never described in detail. No period slurs. Themes of friendship, duty, and hope carry every scene.
ratingOverlays.heroic: Combat has genuine stakes and loss, described with the intensity of a PG-13 war film. Injuries are acknowledged and matter, but the camera never lingers. Grief and fear are shown honestly through people, not through graphic detail.
ratingOverlays.historical: Honest to the era - attrition, gas, shell shock, and the scale of loss are not softened, but nothing is gratuitous or lingered on for shock. The tone honors the people who lived it.
